features.description: Define and manage A/B/C/D check packages, interval triggers (flight hours, cycles, calendar), and task scope per aircraft model and variant
features.reason: Ensures each aircraft adheres to manufacturer-approved maintenance programs and prevents unscheduled groundings

features.description: Capture and accumulate flight hours, landings, and pressurization cycles per airframe, engine, and auxiliary power unit from flight operations data
features.reason: Maintenance intervals are consumption-driven; accurate utilization tracking triggers the right maintenance at the right time

features.description: Project next due dates for scheduled checks based on projected fleet utilization, seasonality, and historical turnaround patterns
features.reason: Enables long-term hangar slot booking, labor resource allocation, and lease return condition planning months in advance

features.description: Auto-generate digital task cards from approved maintenance programs with step-by-step instructions, estimated man-hours, and prerequisite sequencing
features.reason: Standardizes execution across mechanics, eliminates missed steps, and provides baseline data for productivity analysis

features.description: Route tasks to line stations or heavy maintenance hangars based on task complexity, tooling requirements, and aircraft downtime tolerance
features.reason: Optimizes cost and turnaround time by matching task scope to the appropriate execution environment

features.description: Monitor component unscheduled removal rates, mean time between failures, and alert thresholds across the fleet with trend visualization
features.reason: Early detection of systemic reliability issues allows proactive intervention before they affect safety or operational availability

features.description: Track applicability, embodiment status, and compliance deadlines for all regulatory directives and manufacturer bulletins per aircraft tail number
features.reason: Ensures mandatory safety modifications are applied within regulatory deadlines to maintain airworthiness certification

features.description: Track parts with fixed life limits by serial number, monitor remaining hours/cycles/calendar time, and enforce removal before expiry
features.reason: Prevents catastrophic failures and ensures full traceability for regulatory audits and asset resale value

features.description: Manage minimum equipment list items with categorization, expiry tracking, operational restrictions, and rectification workflow
features.reason: Allows continued safe operation with inoperative non-critical equipment while ensuring defects are fixed within permitted timeframes

features.description: Track serialized high-value components through their lifecycle with pool allocation, repair history, and current location across the network
features.reason: Rotables represent the majority of MRO inventory capital; optimized pool management reduces spare asset investment and prevents stockouts

features.description: Manage component repair orders with vendor selection, turnaround time tracking, warranty linkage, and cost accrual per shop visit
features.reason: External repairs are major cost drivers; tracking shop visit performance ensures predictable return-to-service times

features.description: Monitor expiry dates for shelf-life limited materials and calibration due dates for precision tooling with automatic alert generation
features.reason: Prevents use of expired materials or uncalibrated tools which invalidates maintenance records and compromises airworthiness

features.description: Maintain aircraft, engine, and propeller logbooks with digital signatures, immutable revision history, and centralized archival
features.reason: Logbooks are the legal proof of airworthiness; digitization prevents loss and accelerates audit and resale processes

features.description: Generate certificates of release to service with authorized signatory validation, digital stamping, and automatic linkage to completed task records
features.reason: The CRS is the legal document permitting flight after maintenance; invalid or missing certificates ground the aircraft

features.description: Maintain complete component history from original manufacture through all maintenance events, repairs, and installations to current position
features.reason: Complete traceability is required for accident investigation, warranty claims, and maximizing aircraft residual value at sale or lease return

features.description: Track individual mechanic licenses, aircraft type ratings, task authorizations, and recency of experience with automatic expiry alerts
features.reason: Ensures only properly qualified and authorized personnel perform maintenance, preventing regulatory violations and safety risks

features.description: Visualize hangar bay occupancy, plan aircraft input/output sequences, and allocate tooling and floor space per maintenance visit
features.reason: Hangar space is expensive and finite; efficient slot planning maximizes throughput and minimizes aircraft waiting time
